title: Limit Non-Premium Users to One Tableau Project Connection
description:
Restrict non-premium users to a single Tableau project/connection. Premium customers can add multiple Tableau connections.

### Implementation
- New `CheckPremiumForAjax` middleware registered as `ajax.premium`
- Checks the user's current plan to determine premium tier status
- If not premium and the team already has a `TableauCredential` record, the user is blocked from adding more
- If not premium and the team has no `TableauCredential`, the user can add one
- AJAX requests receive a JSON `403` response with a redirect URL (`/not-premium`)
- Non-AJAX requests get a standard redirect to `/not-premium`
acceptance_criteria: Non-premium users can add exactly one Tableau connection
Non-premium users see an upgrade prompt when attempting to add a second connection
Premium users can add multiple Tableau connections
Middleware handles both AJAX and non-AJAX requests appropriately
ux_changes: Non-premium users see an upgrade/restriction screen when trying to add a second Tableau connection.
middleware_changes: New `CheckPremiumForAjax` middleware checks plan tier and existing TableauCredential count per team. Returns 403 with redirect URL for AJAX, standard redirect for non-AJAX.
implementation_notes: Middleware checks existing TableauCredential records for the current team. Premium check uses the user's current plan. This pattern can be extended to other premium-gated features.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the middleware, restricting non-premium users to one Tableau credential per team. AJAX and standard request handling both covered with appropriate redirect behavior.
